body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Agency as inference — the 'I' as nested prediction error minimization, not homunculus.

**Hearing one'sown voice enhances explicit agency retrospectively, whereas implicit agency relies on sensorimotor comparison.**. iScience.. PMID: 41847616. DOI: 10.1016/j.isci.2026.115143. People instantly recognize their own voice during speech, and this self-generated auditory feedback helps shape their sense of agency (SoA), the conscious perception of themselves as agents of their speech acts. Although the role of sensorimotor mechanisms in SoA is well-established, how hearing one'svoice as an outcome of speech influences SoA remains unclear.
